channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Disabled Australians on love, lust and the right to intimacy
item.description: It's a stormy February night in Adelaide, and Jacqueline Tedmanson is at the hospital with her partner Jackson, waiting to meet their first baby.

media:group.media:description: Jacqueline Tedmanson, aka Diana Divine, five months pregnant, still burlesque dancing and showing how mobility aids can be really sexy.

item.title: 'You're malnourished; you are overtrained': Olympic gymnast's warning
item.description: Alexandra Kiroi-Bogatyreva trained up to 10 hours a day, six days a week, often without the proper nutrition. She wants others to know what that can do to the body.
item.dc:creator: Johanna McDiarmid

media:group.media:description: Alexandra Kiroi-Bogatyreva got her first period at 19, and says no one questioned why.

item.title: Why some people pay more to avoid convential home loans
item.description: When Wildan decided to buy a property he didn't even consider a conventional loan. Instead, he opted for a niche option that caters to a growing segment of Australia's population.
item.dc:creator: Raihanah Rasum

media:group.media:description: Wildan Al Hazmi said he chose Islamic financing for "peace of mind".

item.title: Aspiration has changed since the Howard era. This budget is finally catching up
item.description: At the turn of the century, "aspiration" described Australians who wanted to put their kids into private school and university, and to build their wealth by getting an investment property or two. Today, it means something humbler; millions of younger Australians simply aspire to own a home one day, to have some shelter.
item.dc:creator: Gareth Hutchens

media:group.media:description: Prime Minister John Howard and Treasurer Peter Costello hold a press conference on November 18, 2007, a week before they lost the 2007 election.

item.title: Three controversies sparked by Trump's reflecting pool paint job
item.description: Donald Trump has chosen a new colour for Washington DC's Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool. Here are three reasons why it's proving to be controversial.
item.dc:creator: Brad Ryan

media:group.media:description: The Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool will soon be "American flag blue", Donald Trump says.

item.title: As a child Rob was burnt. As an adult, he’s fighting fire with fire
item.description: Every time farmer and volunteer firefighter Rob Armstrong gets a call to battle a bushfire, something inside says not to do it. But he goes anyway. He knows what it’s like to be trapped in the flames and rescued by volunteers.

media:group.media:description: Victorian farmer Rob Armstrong has been a volunteer firefighter for 40 years.

item.title: Will drastic NDIS overhaul repeat the mistakes of the past?
item.description: The states are once again on the hook for disability services due to the Commonwealth's drastic NDIS cuts. Can the mistakes of the past be avoided?
item.dc:creator: Evan Young and Nas Campanella

media:group.media:description: Keith McVilly began working with people with disability in the 1980s, helping people move out of state-run institutions such as Hobart's Willow Court (pictured).

item.title: Rodrigues thought AI would make him rich. Then everything spiralled
item.description: Rodrigues thought an AI chatbot could help him escape poverty and build wealth. Instead, he became trapped in a spiral of delusion and dependence.
item.dc:creator: James Purtill

item.title: Live: Delta Goodrem lights up Eurovision stage
item.description: Delta Goodrem performed eighth in the Eurovision grand final, where she is one of the favourites to win. Follow live.
item.dc:creator: Hanan Dervisevic and Rudi Maxwell

media:group.media:description: Delta Goodrem is representing Australia with her song Eclispe at the 2026 Eurovision Song Contest.

item.title: Artist appointed, axed then reinstated from Venice Biennale triumphs
item.description: Last year, Khaled Sabsabi was appointed, sacked and then reinstated as Australia's representative to the Venice Biennale. Now he opens two installations at the world's most prestigious art exhibition.

media:group.media:description: This year, no best artist or best national pavilion prizes will be awarded after the jury unanimously resigned in late April.

item.title: Rochelle Jordan's new album is so good, even she listens to it
item.description: The Canadian singer won widespread acclaim with her third album. Now she brings it to Australia.
item.dc:creator: Dan Condon

media:group.media:description: Rochelle Jordan returns to Australia this month, bringing one of 2025's most acclaimed albums with her.

item.title: Test your homophones and your Gen Alpha slang
item.description: Are you ready to test your spelling skills like your little lunch depends on it? Come on in.

media:group.media:description: Guy Montgomery on the set of Guy Mont-Spelling Bee for season three.

item.title: Former UK health minister says he will stand in any contest to replace Starmer
item.description: Former health secretary Wes Streeting, who resigned from cabinet on Thursday in preparation for the challenge, says his party needs a "proper contest" with the best candidates on the field.

media:group.media:description: Former UK health secretary Wes Streeting speaks at the Progress annual conference in London.

item.title: Eight dead and 35 injured after train collides with public bus in Bangkok
item.description: Footage of the incident shows a group of vehicles queuing across a railway line, before a cargo train suddenly ploughs into the side of the bus and pushes it into oncoming traffic.
item.dc:creator: Anna Cox

media:group.media:description: The train is seen slamming into the side of the bus then pushing it into oncoming traffic.

item.title: Penalty agony for Newcastle as Sydney FC books place in ALM grand final
item.description: Eli Adams's 96th-minute equaliser proves to be in vain as Sydney FC downs Newcastle on penalties to progress to the A-League Men season decider.

media:group.media:description: Sydney progressed to the season decider with a dramatic penalty shootout victory in Newcastle.

item.title: Labor on track to win by-election in Brisbane seat of Stafford
item.description: At the end of counting on Saturday night, there has been a 4.1 per cent swing against Labor after preferences, with additional postal votes still to be counted.
item.dc:creator: Casey Briggs and Claudia Williams

media:group.media:description: Labor candidate Luke Richmond is on track to win the seat of Stafford.

item.title: Top Hamas military leader killed in Israeli air strikes, official confirms
item.description: Izz al-Din al-Haddad is the most senior Hamas official to be killed by Israel since a US-backed ceasefire agreement meant to halt fighting was implemented in October.

media:group.media:description: A vehicle targeted in one of two Israeli air strikes on Friday intended to kill Hamas's military commander.

item.title: Melbourne City claims ALW championship as Matildas stars shine against Phoenix
item.description: Sensational strikes from young stars Holly McNamara and Leticia McKenna have helped Melbourne City to a record-equalling fifth A-League Women championship.

media:group.media:description: City claimed a fifth championship on Saturday.

item.title: Kohli to play 2027 World Cup only if he can add value
item.description: Virat Kohli says if he plays in the 2027 ICC World Cup he will compete for "every ball like it's the last ball" - but vows if he can't add value to India's team he will not be there.

media:group.media:description: Virat Kohli is only playing in one-day cricket these days, and he says he will only play in next year's World Cup if he is convinced he can add value to India.

item.title: Taylor vows to alter Sex Discrimination Act following 'Tickle v Giggle' ruling
item.description: Opposition Leader Angus Taylor has vowed to amend the Sex Discrimination Act after the Federal Court upheld a landmark ruling that the exclusion of a transgender woman from a female-only app was discriminatory.
item.dc:creator: Lexie Jeuniewic

media:group.media:description: Angus Taylor has vowed to amend Australia’s Sex Discrimination Act to include a definition of biological sex.

item.title: 'Destroys trust in the system': Rally held for Isla Bell
item.description: A rally has been held for Isla Bell in Melbourne after friends and family were left devastated when murder and manslaughter charges were dropped against the man accused of killing the 19-year-old.

media:group.media:description: A vigil was held for 19-year-old Isla Bell, who was found dead in November last year.

item.title: Tasmania to close legal loophole to help child sex abuse survivors
item.description: Tasmania will introduce laws to close a loophole which allows some organisations to avoid being liable for abuse perpetrated by individuals who aren't formal employees.
item.dc:creator: Meg Whitfield

media:group.media:description: Advocate and victim survivor Steve Fisher, from Beyond Abuse, has welcomed Tasmania's newly proposed vicarious liability laws.

item.title: Boy dead, two injured in car crash in north-west Victoria
item.description: Police say a car stolen from Mildura last night was travelling along the Calder Highway when it veered off the road and struck a tree.

media:group.media:description: The crash occurred on the Calder Highway north-west of Bendigo around 10am.

item.title: 'Don't be a d**k, Dezi!': New claims about Freeman police shooting
item.description: The owner of the Porepunkah property where Dezi Freeman shot two police officers says he rang and spoke to Freeman soon after the shooting.
item.dc:creator: Charmaine Manuel

media:group.media:description: Andrew Swift has made new claims in a video recollecting the day police entered his Porepunkah property to arrest Dezi Freeman.

item.title: Origin hopefuls stand up as Cowboys coach hails 'gritty' win
item.description: Todd Payten said his side's victory over the Roosters was "as good a win as I've had" with the club as Melbourne notched back-to-back wins after a messy 34-8 victory over Parramatta and the Sea Eagles thrash Wests Tigers in Magic Round at Lang Park.
item.dc:creator: Simon Smale

media:group.media:description: North Queensland fought back from an early sin binning to stun the Roosters before half time.
